message:
chore: add .mailmap + CITATION.cff + close xgsf/0iyb/tne4 beads (br-29lw + br-8opp)

Two file adds + bead closures in one commit because all three
ships in the same turn landed on branch cod-pandas-zjme-ship and
share a session context.

  frankenpandas-29lw [LOW] .mailmap
    Normalizes swarm author identities (cc-pandas / cod-pandas /
    Clawdstein-libupdater-frankenpandas) to Dicklesworthstone
    canonical. git shortlog now renders a unified contributor
    list instead of the fragmented one.

  frankenpandas-8opp [LOW] CITATION.cff
    Academic-citation metadata. GitHub auto-renders a "Cite this
    repository" button when CFF is present. CFF v1.2.0 format,
    BibTeX/APA auto-generation on demand. Ready for academic
    users who cite frankenpandas in data-science papers (pandas
    itself is cited in tens of thousands of papers/year; frankenpandas
    targets the same audience).

Also closes in JSONL (via the bead tracker):
  frankenpandas-tne4 — #[non_exhaustive] sweep shipped (9b6374d)
  frankenpandas-8k1i — SECURITY.md shipped (fb455f8)
  frankenpandas-0a83 — dead llvm-cov step removed (6005814)
  frankenpandas-0iyb — concurrency group added (b3dda84)
  frankenpandas-xgsf — .editorconfig + .gitattributes shipped

Plus the cod-pandas lxhr slice I finished (040223b —
ci_supply_chain_policy extraction).

Session total (this turn, solo-mode cc-pandas, post-codex-limit):
  7 beads of real code shipped:
    - 1 lxhr slice continuation (cod's WIP)
    - 5 HIGH reviews (tne4, 8k1i, 0a83, 0iyb, xgsf)
    - 2 LOW hygiene (29lw, 8opp — this commit)

  Workspace still 3186/0/13 (+0 after every ship), clippy clean.
  All commits on branch cod-pandas-zjme-ship; ready to merge to
  main or rebase as the orchestrator prefers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pandas-oracle-backed FP-P2D fixtures for single-column rename, partial mapping preservation, ordered multi-rename, and duplicate-target rejection so pandas df.rename(columns=...) parity is frozen.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:59:43.131236525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T03:04:16.848087876Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T03:04:16.846305424Z","close_reason":"Completed DataFrame rename_columns conformance packet coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","dataframe","rename"]}

-{"id":"frankenpandas-0a83","title":"[HIGH] ci: cargo-llvm-cov installed on every run but never invoked — dead step + 0% coverage measured despite claim","description":"Dead-step + no-coverage-measurement audit on ci.yml.

**Observation.** ci.yml has two explicit lines that are currently dead code:

1. Line 37-38:
     - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
       run: cargo install cargo-llvm-cov --locked

   cargo-llvm-cov is installed on every CI run (`cargo install --locked` + cold network fetch + compile ≈ 60-90s each time — no caching of the binary). But the subsequent steps never invoke `cargo llvm-cov` — no `cargo llvm-cov --workspace`, no coverage report generation, no coverage upload, no PR comment. Pure cycle waste + zero coverage data.

2. Line 46:
     - name: Bench harness smoke
       run: cargo bench

   Workspace has no [[bench]] blocks (parallel sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6) so `cargo bench` is a no-op that always passes. The step's purpose was "smoke test that bench infra compiles" but since no bench compiles, it smoke-tests nothing.

**Cumulative CI-time impact:**
- cargo-llvm-cov install (~80s cold, ~30s warm if Cargo cached the source but the install step doesn't cache the binary output between runs).
- cargo bench no-op (~5s on empty-bench workspace).
- Combined: ~1-2 min per CI run, wasted.

Over 50 PRs/week (swarm cadence), that's ~60 CI-minutes wasted weekly. On a paid runner fleet, measurable $; on the self-hosted fleet, CPU contention against real work.

**Severity.** HIGH — not because wasted time is itself HIGH, but because BOTH dead steps create false signals:
- The cargo-llvm-cov install implies someone later wanted coverage — but coverage is 0% measured and 0% enforced. A reader of ci.yml reasonably assumes coverage is measured when it isn't.
- The cargo bench step implies performance is smoke-tested — but nothing is. Same false signal as br-ing6.

**Fix shape:**

For cargo-llvm-cov (this bead):
1. Option A (activate): CI runs `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --lcov --output-path lcov.info` after tests pass. Upload to Codecov / Coveralls. Fail CI when coverage drops > N%. Actionable artifact on every PR.
2. Option B (remove): delete the install step. Zero CI change, truthful ci.yml. Document in a follow-up bead if coverage becomes a priority.

Recommendation: Option A. Coverage data on frankenpandas is genuinely useful — fp-frame has 3836 panic sites (sibling br-7cfm); knowing which ARE exercised by tests narrows the panic-doc backlog.

For cargo bench:
- Covered by sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6 [HIGH: no perf regression gate]. That bead's fix removes or replaces line 46.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+3 lines for llvm-cov invocation + upload), `.github/workflows/ci.yml` env vars for Codecov token, optional README badge. Zero source code changes.

Quick decision: if the team hasn't decided on coverage tracking yet, remove both dead steps today (2 lines) — that's the correct minimum move until a policy lands.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:09.441925853Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:09.441925853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","coverage","dead-code","review"]}

**Observation.** ci.yml has two explicit lines that are currently dead code:

1. Line 37-38:

       run: cargo install cargo-llvm-cov --locked

   cargo-llvm-cov is installed on every CI run (`cargo install --locked` + cold network fetch + compile ≈ 60-90s each time — no caching of the binary). But the subsequent steps never invoke `cargo llvm-cov` — no `cargo llvm-cov --workspace`, no coverage report generation, no coverage upload, no PR comment. Pure cycle waste + zero coverage data.

2. Line 46:
     - name: Bench harness smoke
       run: cargo bench

   Workspace has no [[bench]] blocks (parallel sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6) so `cargo bench` is a no-op that always passes. The step's purpose was "smoke test that bench infra compiles" but since no bench compiles, it smoke-tests nothing.

**Cumulative CI-time impact:**
- cargo-llvm-cov install (~80s cold, ~30s warm if Cargo cached the source but the install step doesn't cache the binary output between runs).
- cargo bench no-op (~5s on empty-bench workspace).
- Combined: ~1-2 min per CI run, wasted.

Over 50 PRs/week (swarm cadence), that's ~60 CI-minutes wasted weekly. On a paid runner fleet, measurable $; on the self-hosted fleet, CPU contention against real work.

**Severity.** HIGH — not because wasted time is itself HIGH, but because BOTH dead steps create false signals:
- The cargo-llvm-cov install implies someone later wanted coverage — but coverage is 0% measured and 0% enforced. A reader of ci.yml reasonably assumes coverage is measured when it isn't.
- The cargo bench step implies performance is smoke-tested — but nothing is. Same false signal as br-ing6.

**Fix shape:**

For cargo-llvm-cov (this bead):
1. Option A (activate): CI runs `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --all-targets --lcov --output-path lcov.info` after tests pass. Upload to Codecov / Coveralls. Fail CI when coverage drops > N%. Actionable artifact on every PR.
2. Option B (remove): delete the install step. Zero CI change, truthful ci.yml. Document in a follow-up bead if coverage becomes a priority.

Recommendation: Option A. Coverage data on frankenpandas is genuinely useful — fp-frame has 3836 panic sites (sibling br-7cfm); knowing which ARE exercised by tests narrows the panic-doc backlog.

For cargo bench:
- Covered by sibling br-frankenpandas-ing6 [HIGH: no perf regression gate]. That bead's fix removes or replaces line 46.

Blast radius: ci.yml (+3 lines for llvm-cov invocation + upload), `.github/workflows/ci.yml` env vars for Codecov token, optional README badge. Zero source code changes.

Quick decision: if the team hasn't decided on coverage tracking yet, remove both dead steps today (2 lines) — that's the correct minimum move until a policy lands.","notes":"cc-pandas 2026-04-23: removed dead cargo-llvm-cov install step from ci.yml via commit 6005814 on branch cod-pandas-zjme-ship. Option A (remove) per the bead's quick-decision recommendation — no coverage policy had landed, install was pure waste. When coverage is activated in future, install step + llvm-cov invocation + upload land as one coordinated commit rather than having the install floating dead.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:00:09.441925853Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T02:32:08.580329998Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T02:32:08.577787264Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","coverage","dead-code","review"]}

+# git-log author normalization for the multi-agent swarm.
+#
+# Format:
+#   Proper Name <proper@email>  Alt Name <alt@email>
+#
+# See: git-mailmap(5).
+
+Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com> cc-pandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>
+Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com> cod-pandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>
+Dicklesworthstone <jeff141421@gmail.com> Clawdstein-libupdater-frankenpandas <jeff141421@gmail.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,32 @@
+cff-version: 1.2.0
+message: "If you use frankenpandas in published research, please cite it."
+title: "frankenpandas: clean-room Rust reimplementation of the pandas API"
+abstract: >
+  frankenpandas rebuilds the pandas API surface in safe Rust with
+  differential conformance testing against the live pandas oracle.
+  Provides DataFrame, Series, GroupBy, Rolling, Resample, MultiIndex,
+  and 7 IO formats (CSV, JSON, JSONL, Parquet, Excel, Feather,
+  Arrow IPC, SQL). Memory safety via #![forbid(unsafe_code)].
+authors:
+  - family-names: "Emanuel"
+    given-names: "Jeffrey"
+repository-code: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenpandas"
+url: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenpandas"
+license: MIT
+version: "0.1.0"
+date-released: "2026-04-23"
+keywords:
+  - dataframe
+  - pandas
+  - rust
+  - data-analysis
+  - columnar-storage
+  - conformance-testing
+preferred-citation:
+  type: software
+  authors:
+    - family-names: "Emanuel"
+      given-names: "Jeffrey"
+  title: "frankenpandas"
+  url: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenpandas"
+  year: 2026
